behavioral_changes.summary: Catalog lookup now treats a cross-lakehouse artifact with an empty ObjectId as corrupted metadata, throwing CatalogException with HTTP 500 and ErrorCode.MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED using a formatted lakehouse/workspace identifier.

Data quality report creation now maps missing lakehouse properties to CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED, and the controller converts that CatalogException into an HTTP error response that preserves the exception status code and message.
Public execution-definition validation now throws a standardized MLV_SELECTED_NOT_FOUND message from ErrorRegistry using the joined missing-MLV names, instead of hardcoded MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND text.

The ErrorRegistry contract for MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND now describes incomplete source metadata for a named materialized lake view, replacing the old generic 'referenced couldn't be found or is inaccessible' wording.

The service adds a first-class MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED error code and standardized ErrorRegistry template that injects a fully qualified MLV/lakehouse identifier and supports standard error-code prefixing.

PySpark DAG creation now consistently converts source-info corruption cases—missing source info, invalid/null parsed source info, malformed JSON, blank required identifiers, and invalid GUIDs—into CatalogException with HTTP 500 and ErrorCode.MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
Catalog table metadata validation now treats empty table names and missing table types as corrupted catalog data, throwing CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of ArgumentException.

grounding_evidence.reason: This replacement is the concrete throw-site that changes invalid lakehouse ObjectId handling from a generic server error to the new corrupted-metadata contract.
This is the new defensive guard that reclassifies null lakehouse properties as corrupted catalog data via CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
This new catch block is the controller behavior that surfaces CatalogException status/message to HTTP callers during data-quality-report creation.
This is the exact validation branch that changes the selected-MLV error payload from hardcoded MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND text to a standardized MLV_SELECTED_NOT_FOUND message built from missing names.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND, $"The materialized lake view '{{{Constants.MLVNameKey}}}' has incomplete source metadata and can't be processed. Update the lake view definition to include a valid source, then try again." },
grounding_evidence.reason: This changed registry entry is the message-contract change for MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
/// Error indicating catalog data is corrupted or missing required information for processing.
MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED,
grounding_evidence.reason: This enum addition creates the new first-class error code used by the updated Catalog, DAG, and Data Quality paths.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED, $"Catalog data does not have information needed to process this request. Recreate the materialized lake view '{{{Constants.FullyQualifiedMLVNameKey}}}' and retry the operation. If issue persists, reach out to support." },
grounding_evidence.reason: This new registry template defines the standardized caller-visible message for corrupted metadata scenarios.
This PySpark-only branch changes the 'missing source info' path from InvalidOperationException to CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
This is the PySpark branch for invalid/null parsed SourceInfo, now mapped to the corrupted-metadata error contract.
This catch-path changes malformed JSON/deserialization failures to CatalogException and preserves the original exception as InnerException.
This guard now treats blank workspaceId/notebookId fields in PySpark SourceInfo as corrupted metadata.
This branch changes invalid GUID parsing in PySpark SourceInfo from InvalidOperationException to CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
This is the new validation/error path for an empty table name during DAG rebuild.
This is the new validation/error path for a missing table type during DAG rebuild.

scenario_sketches.title: Cross-lakehouse catalog lookup with Guid.Empty ObjectId returns MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise the CatalogHandler branch where artifact metadata exists but ObjectId is Guid.Empty, and assert the thrown CatalogException carries HTTP 500, ErrorCode.MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED, and a standardized message naming the lakehouse/workspace.

scenario_sketches.title: Data quality report creation surfaces corrupted lakehouse metadata as controller error response
scenario_sketches.rationale: Drive CreateDataQualityReportAsync through a lakehouse-details response whose Properties is null, then verify the handler throws CatalogException and the controller returns the same status/message in the HTTP response body.

scenario_sketches.title: Selected-MLV validation emits MLV_SELECTED_NOT_FOUND with missing-name list
scenario_sketches.rationale: Validate that invalid selected MLV names now produce the standardized ErrorRegistry-backed error code/message, including the joined missing names in the response instead of the old hardcoded MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND text.

scenario_sketches.title: ErrorRegistry MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND now describes incomplete source metadata
scenario_sketches.rationale: Unit-test the registry message for ErrorCode.MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND to ensure it uses the new materialized-lake-view metadata-corruption wording and the MLVName placeholder, not the old inaccessible-entity wording.

scenario_sketches.title: ErrorRegistry MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED formats fully qualified name and standard prefix
scenario_sketches.rationale: Verify the new MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED registry entry formats the supplied FullyQualifiedMLVName value and, when requested, prepends the standard error-code prefix.

scenario_sketches.title: PySpark DAG creation maps missing or null source-info payloads to CatalogException
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover the PySpark-only guard cases where source info is absent or deserializes to an invalid/null object, and assert both partitions now fail with CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of InvalidOperationException.

scenario_sketches.title: PySpark DAG malformed source-info JSON becomes MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED with inner exception preserved
scenario_sketches.rationale: Pass malformed SourceInfo JSON for a PySpark MLV and assert DAG creation throws CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED with the original deserialization exception attached as InnerException.

scenario_sketches.title: PySpark source-info identifier guards reject blank fields and invalid GUIDs as corrupted metadata
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use a small truth table across blank workspaceId, blank notebookId, and invalid GUID formats to confirm each identifier-validation branch maps to CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.

scenario_sketches.title: DAG rebuild treats empty table name and missing table type as corrupted catalog metadata
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover both metadata-validation guards during DAG rebuild—empty Name and empty TableType—and assert they now raise CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED rather than ArgumentException.
